issues.description:
**As** a Buyer,   
**I want** the ability to add or remove order tracking parameters (order_id & order_value) to pixels (both v1 and v2 versions) inside dsp-trader  
**so** that I can have flexible control over conversion tracking data collection based on my campaign requirements and attribution needs.

  

Business Context

Buyers currently face limitations in their conversion tracking setup when working with pixels. They need the flexibility to customize their tracking implementation based on different campaign types, attribution models, and reporting requirements. Some campaigns may require detailed order-level tracking with specific order IDs and values for precise ROI calculation and performance optimization, while others may only need basic conversion data without the additional parameters.

This flexibility is essential for buyers who manage diverse advertising portfolios with varying tracking needs. Without the ability to dynamically configure these parameters, buyers are forced into a one-size-fits-all approach that may either collect unnecessary data (impacting performance and privacy compliance) or miss critical attribution data needed for campaign optimization and budget allocation decisions.

The solution will enable buyers to make real-time decisions about their tracking setup, improving their operational efficiency and data quality while maintaining compliance with their specific tracking and privacy requirements.

‌

Link to Figma: <custom data-type="smartlink" data-id="id-0">https://www.figma.com/design/5xiK4CQcczrqDXdjtpuCWD/Pixel-Setup?node-id=4111-16476&t=fut0sbxWtu8ENNGJ-0</custom>

‌

Acceptance Criteria:

* Pixel Creation: There should be a checkbox which adds order_id & order_value to pixel
* Pixel Modification: There should be a checkbox which adds or remove order_id & order_value to pixel URL
* The pixel URL is updated after “Update Pixel/Segment” button is clicked

**As** a Buyer,   
**I want** to have the option to select which tag type of standard segment I want to use between segment v1 and v2,   
**so** that I can choose the most appropriate segment implementation based on my specific tracking requirements and technical constraints.

  

Business Context

Buyers currently lack the flexibility to choose between different standard segment tag types when setting up their tracking implementations. They are constrained to use a single segment type without the ability to evaluate and select between v1 and v2 options based on their specific use cases. This limitation prevents buyers from optimizing their segment tracking strategy and forces them into a one-size-fits-all approach. Buyers need the capability to compare and select the segment version that best aligns with their technical infrastructure and business requirements, while maintaining the ability to make informed decisions about which tag type will deliver the best performance for their campaigns.

‌

Link to Figma: <custom data-type="smartlink" data-id="id-0">https://www.figma.com/design/5xiK4CQcczrqDXdjtpuCWD/Pixel-Setup?node-id=4111-16476&t=fut0sbxWtu8ENNGJ-0</custom> 

‌

Acceptance Criteria:

* Everything is hidden behind FF
* User can select tag type of standard segment on UI
* URL is generated based on standard segment tag type
* Inside modification view created pixel cannot be modified to other tag type

    * greyed segment type
    * only one type is seen
    
* Cookie based segment is the default option

issues.summary: Dynamic parameters support for pixels v1
issues.description:
**As** a Buyer,   
**I want** to have the option to add dynamic parameters (order_id and order_value) to existing cookie-based IMG pixels (v1) functionality,   
**so** that I can track conversion data with the same flexibility and capabilities that are currently available in pixel v2 implementation.

 

Business Context

Buyers are currently limited by the cookie-based IMG pixels (v1) implementation which lacks the dynamic parameter functionality they need for comprehensive conversion tracking. They have identified that pixel v2 already supports dynamic parameters like order_id and order_value, but this capability is not available in the v1 cookie-based pixel system. This creates an inconsistent user experience and forces buyers to choose between using the older v1 system (which may have other advantages) and the newer v2 system solely for the dynamic parameter functionality. Buyers need both systems to offer equivalent parameter handling capabilities to make optimal choices based on their specific technical requirements rather than being constrained by feature limitations.

‌

How it was made for v2: <custom data-type="smartlink" data-id="id-0">https://github.com/adgear/rtb-delivery/pull/1129</custom> 

‌

Acceptance Criteria:

* Parametars order_id & order_value are handled for cookie_based pixel (v1) inside rtb-delivery
* Should work the same way as in pixel v2
